acceptance_targets: [chapter_16_f001] In the revised paragraphs beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:243, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:321, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:385, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:425, every sentence must be syntactically complete when read on its own. The reader should no longer encounter emphasis beats that depend on clipped standalone fragments such as "Not a child." or "Mechanical." while the urgency and POV pressure remain intact.

[FBR-005] Pass if a reader entering chapter 16 can understand what object Nami has, why she still has it, and how it connects to the earlier reveal without inventing an off-page replacement. Fail if "I've got the seed" still reads like an impossible resurrection after chapter 14.

[FBR-011] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7248-7252 now makes Bato's recognition legible through a more specific physical or verbal choice and no still/flat wording remains there. Fail if the revised beat is still a generic frozen-face/body reaction.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised paragraphs beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:243, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:321, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:385, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:425, every sentence must be syntactically complete when read on its own. The reader should no longer encounter emphasis beats that depend on clipped standalone fragments such as "Not a child." or "Mechanical." while the urgency and POV pressure remain intact.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly leans on clipped fragment beats such as "Not a child.", "Not identical.", "Not conviction anymore.", and "Not holy. Mechanical." This is not just a stylistic preference issue; the constitution explicitly forbids elliptical clipping in narration, and the recurrence turns high-pressure moments into a recognizably mechanical house tic instead of earned compression.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the paragraphs beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:243, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:321, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:385, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:425 so the emphasis lands in grammatically complete sentences from the active POV. Keep the force, but replace the orphaned corrective fragments with full-syntax observations, judgments, or action consequences specific to Riva's or Iven's consciousness.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader entering chapter 16 can understand what object Nami has, why she still has it, and how it connects to the earlier reveal without inventing an off-page replacement. Fail if "I've got the seed" still reads like an impossible resurrection after chapter 14.
findings.problem: Chapter 16 announces "I've got the seed" and visualizes a hidden seed in Nami's pocket after chapter 14 has already split that object open. Even if chapter 14 is repaired, this chapter currently relies on the reader silently supplying a resurrected or replacement seed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the object-state at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7436-7444 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7460. Make it explicit that Nami is referring to the same surviving salt-fig seed she still carries after Bato's warning, not a new or magically restored object; if this scene does not need the seed materially, replace the claim with another already-established contribution. The chapter must not rely on an unexplained return of the seed.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7248-7252 now makes Bato's recognition legible through a more specific physical or verbal choice and no still/flat wording remains there. Fail if the revised beat is still a generic frozen-face/body reaction.
findings.problem: Chapter 16 adds another entry to the novel's repeated still/flat reaction pattern when Bato "went still." Because the book already uses this construction across six chapters, the line reads as default shorthand rather than the precise recognition beat this reveal deserves.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE the reaction at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7248-7252 with a more exact sign of Bato's recognition, history, or alarm that fits his spare register without falling back on the stock stillness template.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the collision of sanctity, lust, labor, rot, and family resentment. In edge cases, choose the more concrete, uncomfortable, and character-specific rendering over symbolic safety; if an image or action feels too polite for the pressure, it is probably wrong.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, violence, shame, grief, or economic pressure drives a scene, vague language counts as a failed beat; replace euphemism with the exact bodily, social, or material consequence the character experiences.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm across dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for ritual speech, emphasis, or deliberate character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative movement aggressively: follow ordeal with meal, revelation with labor, schism with care, and public spectacle with close-bodied aftermath so no adjacent chapters share the same structural rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, bodily, locally sacred, and unsanitized; lyrical only when pressure earns it, never as a decorative default.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person rotating among the five siblings; each scene stays inside one consciousness and lets the river press through perception rather than omniscient explanation.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate muscular propulsion with denser lyric accumulation; peak scenes should tighten and clarify rather than dissolve into fragmentary haze.
